pages.text: The Storage Core currently maintains $(thing)8 energy pools$(). Energy powers smelting, blasting, smoking, and campfire cooking through the Crafting Terminal. Brewing process energy plus Brew and Bottle fuel reserves can be stockpiled, but brewing execution is not implemented yet.

$(thing)Installed process stations:$()$(br)$(li)Furnace → Smelting Energy$(li)Blast Furnace → Blasting Energy$(li)Smoker → Smoking Energy$(li)Campfire → Campfire Energy$(li)Brewing Stand → Brewing Energy$(br2)An empty slot generates nothing. A stack of N energy machines produces exactly N matching energy per tick. Crafting Table, Stonecutter, Smithing Table, and axe slots unlock recipes/actions but produce no energy.

$(thing)Fuel-conversion pools:$()$(br)$(li)$(thing)Fuel$() - every stack whose current NeoForge runtime burn time is positive, including logs and modded fuels$(li)$(thing)Brew Energy$() - Blaze Rods/Powder$(li)$(thing)Bottle Energy$() - Glass Bottles/Potions$(br2)These labels do not change the save-compatible internal pool IDs.

All energy and installed station/tool stacks live in the Storage Core's $(thing)NBT data$() and survive saves and the data-carrying Core drop. Removing a process machine stops future generation but keeps stored energy.$(br2)The Crafting Terminal $(thing)Fuel page$() displays all currently registered totals live; scroll inside a panel when its page indicator appears. See $(l:magic_storage:fuel_conversion)Fuel Conversion$() and $(l:magic_storage:recipe_costs)Recipe Costs$().
